questions.question: Colab Pro upgrades provide:
options: Access to A100/V100 GPUs and 24-hour session limits vs. T4 and 12 hours free
questions.explanation: Colab Pro = better GPUs + longer sessions + more RAM.

questions.question: Google Drive mounting in Colab is needed for:
options: Persisting files across sessions -- Colab's /content/ is reset on disconnect
questions.explanation: drive.mount('/content/drive') is the first cell in most Colab notebooks.

questions.question: Colab runtime disconnect prevention:
options: Running Keep Alive script in browser console or using Colab Pro for longer sessions
questions.explanation: Free tier disconnects after ~90 min of inactivity. Save checkpoints frequently.

questions.question: !nvidia-smi in Colab shows:
options: GPU name, VRAM total/used, temperature, and running CUDA processes
questions.explanation: Run this first to confirm you have GPU access.

questions.question: Colab vs Kaggle for internet access:
options: Both work, but Colab has always-on internet while Kaggle requires toggling the internet setting
questions.explanation: Either works. Kaggle: Settings->Internet->On. Colab: internet always enabled.

questions.question: The recommended way to install packages in Colab:
questions.explanation: pip is standard. -q keeps notebook clean. Packages persist for the session only.

questions.question: To share a Colab notebook:
options: Share via Google Drive link or File -> Save to GitHub
questions.explanation: Colab notebooks are Google Docs -- share link controls access.

questions.question: Colab T4 vs Kaggle P100:
options: Similar performance. P100 has faster HBM2 memory but T4 has tensor cores for fp16
questions.explanation: P100 = higher bandwidth. T4 = better for fp16/int8 workloads.

questions.question: After Colab disconnects, you lose:
options: All files in /content/ not in Drive, installed packages, and running processes
questions.explanation: Save model checkpoints to Drive and log metrics to W&B/MLflow to protect against disconnects.

questions.question: TPU runtime in Colab is best for:
options: Large matrix multiplications -- especially training big NLP/vision models with TensorFlow/JAX
questions.explanation: TPUs excel at large batch training of models that fit the TPU topology.
